page.content: The table presents data on gender and various sexual health metrics. For the total gender count, there are 56 males and 64 females, yielding a total of 120 respondents, which is 100.0%. In the category of MSM or WSM, the count is 5 males and 3 females, totaling 5 respondents, which represents 7.7%. For MSW or WSM, there are 51 males and 61 females, totaling 112 respondents at 93.3%. Among those born in Australia, 31 are male and 45 are female, resulting in a total of 76 individuals, which is 63.3%. In the category of sex with a new partner for over 12 months, the data shows 40 males and 35 females, totaling 75 respondents (*115), corresponding to 65.2%.

page.content: For sex with a new partner from overseas for over 12 months, there are 22 males and 5 females, totaling 27 respondents at 23.5%. Regarding sex with someone with an STI for over 12 months, the count is 1 male and 1 female, totaling 2 respondents, which is 1.6%. The data shows 1 male and 2 females have injected drugs not prescribed by a GP, totaling 3 respondents at 2.5%. Additionally, 31 males (*53) and 47 females (*63) do not use condoms 100% of the time, totaling 78 respondents at 67.2%. Finally, 23 males (*53) and 24 females reported having sex with a new casual partner while also reporting not using condoms 100% of the time, totaling 47 respondents (*115), which is 40.5%.
